spec_match.rationale: Exercises anchor-size() in inset, margin, sizing, and calc() inset expressions: `bottom: anchor(top)`, `margin-bottom: anchor-size(height)`, `width: anchor-size(width)`, and proportional `calc(anchor(...) + anchor-size(...))` placement.

interactive.rationale: Slider rescales the anchor live, the primary tooltip and calc tooltip update their measured geometry, and a fallback-preview toggle lets visitors compare the native @supports path with the unsupported-browser layout.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature card promises 'anchor-size() usable in inset/margin properties' — exactly delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP verified native support, fallback-preview mode, and an emulated unsupported browser: the page shows a visible no-support banner, applies the fallback class, keeps the tooltip sized to the anchor, and avoids a top-left origin drop.

references.rationale: See also links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5203950077476864 and the CSS spec.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables throughout, button-independent toggle styling sets readable text/background pairs, no raw colors were introduced, and the page remains an interactive demo rather than a static code card.

summary: The anchor-size inset demo now gates the native layout with @supports, ships a centered fallback path with an explicit no-support banner, includes a fallback-preview control for supported browsers, and adds a second tooltip demonstrating multiplicative calc() positioning. Tested through Chrome DevTools MCP on the native page, the fallback-preview toggle, an emulated unsupported CSS.supports path, and the feature conformance route.
